item.title: Firewall creation failure leaves provisioned VM with unrestricted inbound traffic — no abort
item.intent: A provisioned agent must never reach the user in an insecure state. Firewall failure is fatal — the VM must be destroyed and the user must be told to retry.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts line 184: when adapter.createFirewall() fails, the engine logs a warning ('Inbound traffic is unrestricted') and continues, leaving the VM running without firewall protection. The instance is then registered as active in an insecure state. Users who see this warning may not understand that all ports are open to the internet. Fix: on firewall failure, abort provisioning, destroy the VM, and return a ProvisionError. A VM without firewall must not be registered as an active instance.
acceptanceCriteria: Firewall creation failure causes provisioning to abort and destroy the VM — no active instance is registered
User receives a clear error message that firewall failed and the VM was cleaned up automatically
No VM remains running after a firewall failure — verified by checking provider console
